2605.30102.title: When Cloud Agents Meet Device Agents: Lessons from Hybrid Multi-Agent Systems
2605.30102.paper_summary: The design space of agentic AI inference spans two extremes: frontier large language models (LLMs), typically hosted in the cloud and offering strong performance across a wide range of tasks at substantially high cost, and more cost-efficient small language models (SLMs), which are amenable to on-device inference. Hybrid multi-agent systems (MASs) combining on-device and cloud models offer a promising middle ground, but they also introduce a complex and poorly understood design space in which task accuracy, monetary cost, and edge energy consumption are tightly coupled; in the absence of general design principles, hybrid components, although not the most prevalent choice, are typically introduced through ad hoc decisions tailored to specific domains. In this work, we examine this design space more systematically. We adapt two representative MAS architectures to support hybrid inference and study how individual design choices shift the operating point along the Pareto frontier of power, cost, and performance. Our findings paint a nuanced picture of hybrid MAS design: while SLMs can effectively benefit from LLM assistance, the optimal architecture is highly task-dependent, and greater frontier-level compute does not consistently translate to better performance.
2605.30102.paper_summary_zh: 代理人工智慧推論的設計空間涵蓋了兩個極端：前沿的大型語言模型（LLMs），通常托管在雲端，並在廣泛的任務中提供強大的性能，但成本相當高；以及更具成本效益的小型語言模型（SLMs），適合在設備上進行推論。結合設備和雲端模型的混合多代理系統（MASs）提供了一個有前景的中間選擇，但它們也引入了一個複雜且不易理解的設計空間，在這個空間中，任務準確性、金錢成本和邊緣能耗緊密相連；在缺乏一般設計原則的情況下，混合組件雖然不是最普遍的選擇，通常是通過針對特定領域的臨時決策引入的。在本研究中，我們更系統地檢視這個設計空間。我們調整了兩個代表性的MAS架構以支持混合推論，並研究個別設計選擇如何改變在功率、成本和性能的帕累托邊界上的操作點。我們的發現描繪了混合MAS設計的細緻圖景：雖然SLMs可以有效地受益於LLM的協助，但最佳架構高度依賴於任務，且更高的前沿計算不一定會持續轉化為更好的性能。
2605.30102.author: Corrado Rainone et.al.
2605.30102.authors: Corrado Rainone, Davide Belli, Bence Major, Arash Behboodi
